title: scaevola taccada f. moomomiana
intro: Scaevola taccada f. moomomiana, also known as the Island Blue Sage, is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, and it is also used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Scaevola taccada f. moomomiana is often made into a tea, salve, or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its therapeutic properties.
